intitule: Exercise 1: Vocabulary – Jobs and Common Tasks

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the descriptions below and write the name of the job that corresponds to each description. Choose from the words in the box.

Word Box: teacher, nurse, mechanic, driver, cook, farmer, carpenter, police officer

questions.texte: 1. This person works in a hospital and takes care of sick people.
2. This person grows crops and raises animals.
3. This person prepares meals in a restaurant or hotel.
4. This person teaches students in a school.
5. This person repairs cars and other vehicles.

intitule: Exercise 2: Reading Comprehension – Common Tasks Associated with Jobs

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the text below and answer the questions that follow.

A Day in the Life of a Teacher

Mr. Mbella is a teacher at Government High School. Every day, he wakes up at 5:30 a.m. He prepares his lessons and checks his students' homework. At 7:00 a.m., he leaves for school. He arrives at 7:30 and goes to the staff room. At 8:00, he goes to his classroom and teaches English to his students. He explains grammar rules, reads stories, and helps students with their writing. During break time, he corrects exercises and talks with other teachers. In the afternoon, he marks tests and prepares the next day's lessons. Mr. Mbella loves his job because he enjoys helping young people learn.

questions.texte: 1. Identify the job of Mr. Mbella.
2. List three tasks Mr. Mbella does in the morning.
questions.reponse: Answer: Three tasks are: (1) preparing lessons, (2) checking homework, and (3) teaching English to his students.

questions.texte: 3. Describe what Mr. Mbella does during break time.
questions.reponse: Answer: During break time, Mr. Mbella corrects exercises and talks with other teachers.

questions.texte: 4. Explain why Mr. Mbella loves his job.
questions.reponse: Answer: Mr. Mbella loves his job because he enjoys helping young people learn.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Complete the sentences using the present continuous tense of the verbs in brackets.

questions.texte: 1. The mechanic ________ (repair) the car right now.
2. The students ________ (write) their essays in the classroom.
3. She ________ (prepare) dinner for her family at the moment.
4. They ________ (build) a new bridge near the village.
5. I ________ (study) for my final exams this week.

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Choose a job that you know well (e.g., your parent's job, a teacher's job, a farmer's job). Write a short paragraph (about 60-80 words) describing the tasks associated with that job. Use the present continuous tense to describe what the person is doing right now or at specific times. Include at least:

  • What the job is
  • Two tasks performed regularly
  • One task the person is doing right now

questions.texte: 1. Write a short paragraph describing a job and its tasks.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:
My mother is a nurse. She works at the regional hospital. Every morning, she checks on patients and gives them medicine. She also writes reports about their health. Right now, she is taking care of a sick child. She is talking to the child's mother and explaining the treatment. My mother works very hard because she wants to help people get better. I am proud of her.

intitule: Exercise 5: Problem-Solving – Organizing a Career Day at School

enonce.texte:
Scenario: Your school is organizing a Career Day to help students learn about different jobs and professions. The event will take place next Saturday. As a member of the student organizing committee, you need to plan the activities. The principal has given you a budget of 200,000 CFA. Below is a list of expenses and tasks. Your team must organize the event and solve the problems.

Expense List:

  • Printing posters and flyers: 30,000 CFA
  • Renting chairs and tables: 40,000 CFA
  • Refreshments for 50 participants: 50,000 CFA
  • Invited speakers' transport: 60,000 CFA
  • Sound system rental: 30,000 CFA
  • Certificates for speakers: 20,000 CFA
images: A school hall with tables and chairs arranged for an event. Students are sitting and listening to a speaker on stage. Posters about different careers are displayed on the walls.

questions.texte: 1. Calculate the total cost of all items on the expense list.
2. Analyze the budget situation. Is the budget enough to cover all expenses? If not, what changes would you suggest to make it work?
questions.reponse: Answer: The budget is not enough. The total is 230,000 CFA, which is 30,000 CFA more than the allocated budget. I would suggest: 1) Asking students to bring their own chairs and tables from home (saving 40,000 CFA). 2) Reducing the number of certificates from 20,000 to 10,000 CFA (saving 10,000 CFA). This would bring the total to 180,000 CFA, which is within the budget.

questions.texte: 3. Propose a program for the Career Day. Include at least 4 activities and explain how each activity helps students learn about jobs.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:
9:00 a.m. – Opening Ceremony: Welcome speech by the principal.
9:30 a.m. – Panel Discussion: Speakers from different professions talk about their jobs and tasks.
11:00 a.m. – Workshop Sessions: Students visit stations to learn specific skills (e.g., writing a CV, preparing for an interview).
12:30 p.m. – Career Fair: Speakers share information about job availability and demand.
1:30 p.m. – Closing Ceremony: Awards for speakers.

questions.texte: 4. Justify why it is important to include a workshop session on writing a CV and preparing for interviews.
questions.reponse: Answer: Including a workshop on writing a CV and preparing for interviews is important because it helps students develop practical skills they will need when applying for jobs. Many students do not know how to present themselves professionally. This workshop builds their confidence and gives them tools to succeed in the job market. It also connects classroom learning to real-world career opportunities.
